name: cf-ask

criteria.name: answer_accuracy
criteria.description: Answer is factually correct based on the actual codebase state

scoring.0: Answer is wrong — states facts that contradict the codebase
scoring.1: Answer is partially correct but contains a significant inaccuracy
scoring.2: Answer is correct on main points but has a minor inaccuracy or outdated detail
scoring.3: Answer is fully accurate and consistent with current codebase state

criteria.name: conciseness
criteria.description: Answer is appropriately concise for a quick Q&A interaction

scoring.0: Answer is a wall of text that buries the actual answer in irrelevant detail
scoring.1: Answer includes the right information but is 3x longer than needed
scoring.2: Answer is mostly concise but has some unnecessary elaboration
scoring.3: Answer is direct, focused, and exactly as long as needed — no more, no less

criteria.name: source_references
criteria.description: Answer points to specific files and locations that support it

scoring.0: No file references — answer seems to come from nowhere
scoring.1: References a directory but not specific files
scoring.2: References specific files but not the most relevant ones
scoring.3: References the exact files and line ranges where the answer can be verified

criteria.name: memory_saved
criteria.description: Answer is saved to memory for future reference when appropriate

scoring.0: Question warranted saving but nothing was saved to docs/memory/
scoring.1: Memory file created but content is too sparse to be useful later
scoring.2: Memory file created with good content but title or location could be better
scoring.3: Memory file created with clear title, concise content, and proper categorization

notes: Not all questions warrant saving to memory — trivial or one-off questions should not be saved. Score memory_saved as 3 if the question genuinely does not need to be remembered and no file was created.
